title: Unchecked `PyDict_New()` in function wrapper call paths can mask `MemoryError`
author: GrahamDumpleton
body:
## Summary

Three call sites in `src/wrapt/_wrappers.c` allocate an empty kwargs dict via
`PyDict_New()` without checking for failure. On allocation failure the `NULL`
return is then passed as the `kwds` argument to `PyObject_CallFunctionObjArgs`,
whose variadic argument list is `NULL`-terminated. This truncates the call,
masking the original `MemoryError` and producing a misleading `TypeError` from
the wrapper signature mismatch instead.

## Credits

Thanks to @devdanzin for the report via:

* https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/e1ac0c43dc4d4278ee82455da4859872
